# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.125.0/http/server.ts";
const port = 8000; // default is 3000, but can be set to any available unoccupied TCP Port (e.g., 4976) for Deno Deploy's edge runtime environment or use a different one if you want the app running on that specific port
const modulesDirectory = "./modules/"; // directory where your custom module files are located, relative to this file in which it is run (e.g., ./my-custom-module/) 
let loadedModules: { [key: string]: any } = {};

serve(async (req) => {
    const urlPathname = `${new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost').pathname}`; // get the path from request object for example '/my-custom-module/index' or '' 
    
    if (!loadedModules[urlPathname]) {  
        try {                  
            const module = await import(modulesDirectory + urlPathname);          
             loadedModules[`${new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost').pathname}`] =  new (module as any).default;  // default export or named exports if they exist  
        } catch {                 
            return new Response("Module not found", { status: 404 });   
        }    
      }             
       const module = loadedModules[urlPathname];            
          try{                   
               await (module as any).default();                // call default method if exists, otherwise do nothing  
           }catch(e){                                  
            console.error("Error running custom function", e); 
        }} , { port });    }) ;
